task.title: Creating an MVS subproject
task.shortdesc: MVS subprojects contain the development resources on an MVS system. You can create multiple subprojects in a z/OS project.

taskbody.prereq: Before you create an MVS subproject, you must create a z/OS project.

step.cmd: From the view menu, click NewMVS Subproject .
step.stepresult: The New MVS Subproject wizard opens. The Connection name field shows the name of the z/OS system to which you are connected.

step.cmd: Do one of the following steps:

choices.choice: If you are connected to more than one system, change the system under which the subproject is created by selecting the system from the Connection name list.

If you are not connected to a remote system, you can create a connection by selecting Create Connection from the Connection name list. The Remote z/OS System Connection wizard opens. For instructions about using this wizard, see the related links. After the wizard completes, it returns you to the New MVS Subproject wizard.

step.cmd: From the Project name list, select the z/OS project to contain this subproject.
step.info: This field defaults to the project name selected when you clicked the New MVS Subproject action.

step.cmd: In the Subproject name field, type a name for the subproject.
step.info: The High-level qualifier defaults to the user ID with which you logged on to the remote system.

step.cmd: You can choose a different high-level qualifier from the High-level qualifier list.

info.note: You can also change the high-level qualifier after the subproject is created. For more information about how to change a high-level qualifier, see the related topics.

step.cmd: In the Runtime environment field, select a runtime environment to associate with the subproject.
step.info:
The runtime environment is used to associate runtime-specific actions with projects and subprojects. You can choose from the following runtime environments:
Any: Enables all project and subproject actions. This runtime environment is the default.
Batch: This option is reserved for future use.
CICS: Enables CICS actions and disables other runtime-specific actions.
IMS: This option is reserved for future use.

step.cmd: To add resources to the subproject after it is created, select the Select and add resources to the subproject checkbox.
step.stepresult: Selecting this option causes the Add Resources to Subproject window to open after the subproject is created. To prevent this window from opening, clear this checkbox. You can add resources to the subproject later.

step.cmd: From the Property group area, choose one of the following options:

choices.choice: Select a property group to associate with the subproject: Select this option and then select a property group for the subproject. You can sort the list by clicking the table headings. To reverse the sort order, click the table headings again.

Create a property group and associate it with the subproject: Select this option to create a property group for the subproject. Type a name for the group in the Name field, choose a language from the Application language list, and click Finish to set values in the property group editor. The default application language is COBOL.
Do not associate a property group with the subproject: Select this option to create the subproject without associating a property group with it.

info.note: A resource in the Remote Systems view can have a different property group than the same physical resource in the view. z/OS Project resources are logical representations of the actual physical resource and therefore each can have its own property group setting. Setting a property group in one location does not associate the same property group to the resource in all locations.

step.cmd: Click Finish to create the subproject and associate the property group.
step.stepresult: If you created a remote system connection, the wizard prompts you to log on to the system.

image.alt: Animation illustrating how to create a subproject

taskbody.postreq: If you choose not to associate a property group with the subproject, you can make an association later. To associate a property group with the subproject, select the subproject and click Property GroupAssociate Property Group.
